ogTitle: Hickeys Creek vs High Range: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hickeys Creek and High Range.
heading: Hickeys Creek vs High Range — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hickeys Creek, New South Wales and High Range, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: High Range has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Hickeys Creek (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hickeys Creek or High Range safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hickeys Creek has a flood risk score of 23/100, while High Range scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hickeys Creek scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. High Range scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hickeys Creek or High Range?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. High Range has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
